questions.question: How many players are on the field for a single football team during a standard match?
questions.explanation: A standard football team has exactly 11 players on the pitch, including the goalkeeper.

questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cups in history?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002).

questions.question: What is the duration of a standard football match, excluding extra time?
questions.explanation: A standard association football match consists of two halves of 45 minutes, totaling 90 minutes.

questions.question: Who is the only player to have won three FIFA World Cup tournaments?
questions.explanation: Pelé won three World Cups with Brazil, in 1958, 1962, and 1970.

questions.question: In which country was the first ever FIFA World Cup held in 1930?
questions.explanation: The inaugural FIFA World Cup was hosted and won by Uruguay in 1930.

questions.question: What is the offside rule in football?
options: A. Being in the opponent's half
B. Closer to the opponent's goal line than the ball and second-last opponent when played
D. Touching the ball with hands
questions.explanation: A player is caught offside if they are closer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-last opponent when the ball is passed to them.

questions.question: Which club won the first five editions of the European Cup (now UEFA Champions League) from 1956 to 1960?
questions.explanation: Real Madrid dominated the early years of European football, winning the first five consecutive titles.

questions.question: In which year did Lionel Messi score a record-breaking 91 goals in a single calendar year?
questions.explanation: Lionel Messi set an all-time world record by scoring 91 goals for Barcelona and Argentina in 2012.

questions.question: Which player holds the record for the most Ballon d'Or awards in football history?
questions.explanation: Lionel Messi has won a record 8 Ballon d'Or awards, the most of any player in football history.

questions.question: The modern rules of association football were first codified in 1863 with the founding of which organization?
questions.explanation: The modern rules of association football were first codified in England in 1863 with the founding of the Football Association (FA).

questions.question: Which team has won the UEFA Champions League tournament the most times?
questions.explanation: Real Madrid holds the record for the most UEFA Champions League titles, having won it 15 times.

questions.question: What happens when a player receives a direct red card in football?
options: B. They sit out for 10 minutes
C. They are ejected immediately and cannot be replaced
D. Their team loses a goal
questions.explanation: A red card results in immediate ejection from the field, and the team must play the rest of the game with 10 players.

questions.question: What is the length of a standard cricket pitch between the wickets?
questions.explanation: A standard cricket pitch is exactly 22 yards (20.12 meters) in length.

questions.question: How many players are in a standard cricket team on the field?
questions.explanation: A standard cricket team consists of 11 players.

questions.question: Which country won the first-ever Cricket World Cup held in 1975?
questions.explanation: The first Cricket World Cup was held in England in 1975 and was won by the West Indies.

questions.question: Who is the only player in cricket history to score 100 international centuries?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar of India is the only player in international cricket history to score 100 international centuries.

questions.question: Who holds the record for the highest individual Test batting average of 99.94?
questions.explanation: Sir Don Bradman of Australia holds the highest individual Test batting average of 99.94.

questions.question: In which year was the first official Test match played, and between which two teams?
questions.explanation: The first official Test match was played in 1877 between England and Australia in Melbourne, which Australia won.

questions.question: How many different ways can a batsman be dismissed by an umpire under standard cricket rules?
questions.explanation: An umpire can dismiss a batsman in 10 different ways, including bowled, caught, LBW (Leg Before Wicket), run out, stumped, and hit wicket.

questions.question: Who holds the record for the most wickets in Test cricket history with exactly 800 wickets?
questions.explanation: Muttiah Muralitharan of Sri Lanka holds the record for the most wickets in Test (800) and ODI (534) cricket.

questions.question: The famous 'Ashes' series between England and Australia was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The 'Ashes' series between England and Australia was established in 1882 after a mock obituary for English cricket was published.

questions.question: In 1844, the first official international cricket match was played between which two countries?
questions.explanation: The first official international cricket match was played in 1844 between the United States and Canada.

questions.question: Which country won the ICC Test Championship Mace for the inaugural 2019-2021 cycle?
questions.explanation: New Zealand won the inaugural ICC World Test Championship by defeating India in the final at Southampton in June 2021.

questions.question: Who was the first cricket player to score a double century in an One Day International (ODI) match?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar of India became the first player to score a double century in men's ODI history, scoring 200* against South Africa in 2010.

questions.question: Who invented the game of basketball in December 1891?
questions.explanation: Basketball was invented in December 1891 by Dr. James Naismith, a Canadian physical education instructor, in Springfield, Massachusetts.

questions.question: How many players are on the court for a single team during a standard basketball game?
questions.explanation: A standard basketball team has 5 players on the court.

questions.question: How many points is a standard basket worth if shot from within the three-point arc?
questions.explanation: A basket made from within the three-point arc is worth 2 points, while a basket made from beyond the arc is worth 3 points.

questions.question: How many points is a successfully converted free throw worth?
questions.explanation: A free throw is worth exactly 1 point.

questions.question: Which two NBA franchises are tied for the most championships in league history with 17 titles each?
options: A. Chicago Bulls & Golden State Warriors
B. Boston Celtics & Los Angeles Lakers
C. Miami Heat & San Antonio Spurs
questions.explanation: The Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers are tied for the most NBA championships, with 17 titles each.

questions.question: In which year was the National Basketball Association (NBA) originally founded?
questions.explanation: The National Basketball Association (NBA) was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America (BAA).

questions.question: What is the violation called when a player stops dribbling, holds the ball, and then starts dribbling again?
questions.explanation: Dribbling the ball with both hands simultaneously or stopping and starting dribbling again constitutes a 'double dribble' violation.

questions.question: Who surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in 2023 to become the NBA's all-time leading scorer?
questions.explanation: LeBron James is the NBA's all-time leading scorer, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's long-standing record of 38,387 points in 2023.

questions.question: Which legendary player scored an NBA-record 100 points in a single game on March 2, 1962?
questions.explanation: Wilt Chamberlain scored an NBA-record 100 points in a single game on March 2, 1962, playing for the Philadelphia Warriors against the New York Knicks.

questions.question: How many NBA championships did Michael Jordan win with the Chicago Bulls, and how many Finals MVP awards did he receive?
questions.explanation: Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls, winning Finals MVP in all six.

questions.question: Which NBA player holds the record for the most career assists in league history?
questions.explanation: John Stockton holds the all-time NBA record for assists (15,806) and steals (3,265) by a wide margin.

questions.question: What is the standard height of an NBA basketball hoop from the gym floor?
questions.explanation: The standard height of a basketball rim is exactly 10 feet (3.05 meters) above the playing surface.

questions.question: What is the oldest and most prestigious tennis tournament in the world, founded in 1877?
questions.explanation: The Wimbledon Championships, founded in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world.

questions.question: How many points does a player score when they win the first point of a game?
questions.explanation: Tennis scoring follows a traditional sequence: Love (0), 15, 30, 40, Game.

questions.question: What is the term used in tennis scoring to represent a score of zero?
questions.explanation: In tennis, 'Love' represents a score of zero.

questions.question: How many serves is a tennis player allowed to make per point before losing the point?
questions.explanation: Players serve diagonally into the opponent's service box, and are allowed two serves (first serve and second serve) per point.

questions.question: Who was the first male player in history to reach 20 Grand Slam singles titles?
questions.explanation: Roger Federer was the first male player to reach 20 Grand Slam singles titles.

questions.question: Who is known as the 'King of Clay' and holds a record 14 French Open singles titles?
questions.explanation: Rafael Nadal is known as the 'King of Clay' and holds a record 14 French Open singles titles.

questions.question: What is the tennis scoring term used when the game score is tied at 40-40?
questions.explanation: If tied at 40-40, it is called 'Deuce', requiring a two-point margin to win.

questions.question: Who holds the Open Era record for the most Grand Slam singles titles in women's tennis with 23?
questions.explanation: Serena Williams holds the Open Era record for the most Grand Slam singles titles with 23.

questions.question: Who is the only player to achieve the 'Golden Slam' by winning all four Grand Slams and an Olympic Gold medal in a single calendar year?
questions.explanation: Steffi Graf is the only player to achieve the 'Golden Slam' by winning all four Grand Slams and an Olympic Gold medal in a single calendar year (1988).

questions.question: What are the exact dimensions of a standard tennis court for singles matches?
options: A. 78 feet long and 27 feet wide
B. 80 feet long and 30 feet wide
C. 75 feet long and 25 feet wide
D. 78 feet long and 36 feet wide
questions.explanation: A standard tennis court measures 78 feet (23.77 meters) long and 27 feet (8.23 meters) wide for singles matches.

questions.question: Which player holds the all-time record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history?
questions.explanation: Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history.

questions.question: Which Grand Slam tournament is played on traditional grass courts?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is famous for being the only Grand Slam tournament played on traditional grass courts.

questions.question: What is the projectile used in the game of badminton called?
questions.explanation: A match is played with a shuttlecock (commonly referred to as a birdie) and lightweight rackets.

questions.question: How many points must a player or pair score to win a standard game of badminton?
questions.explanation: The first player/pair to reach 21 points wins a game under standard rally scoring rules.

questions.question: What is the rule when a shuttlecock lands directly on any of the boundary lines?
options: A. It is out of bounds
C. The point must be replayed
questions.explanation: A shuttlecock landing on a boundary line is considered in-bounds.

questions.question: From what height must a badminton serve be hit under standard rules?
options: B. Underhand, from below the waist level
questions.explanation: The serve must be hit underhand from below the server's waist level.

questions.question: Where did badminton develop in the mid-19th century from an earlier game called battledore and shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: Badminton developed in British India in the mid-19th century from an earlier game called battledore and shuttlecock.

questions.question: In which year did Badminton make its official debut as an Olympic medal sport?
questions.explanation: Badminton made its debut as an official Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Games.

questions.question: If the score is tied at 20-20 in badminton, how many points must a side win by to secure the game?
questions.explanation: If the score is 20-20, a side must win by 2 points (e.g., 22-20) up to a maximum cap of 30 points (where 30-29 wins).

questions.question: Which country has historically dominated badminton, winning the most Olympic gold medals and Uber/Thomas Cup titles?
questions.explanation: China has historically dominated badminton, winning the most Olympic gold medals and Uber/Thomas Cup titles.

questions.question: Which legendary player is widely considered the greatest singles badminton player ever, holding two Olympic golds and five World Championships?
questions.explanation: Lin Dan of China is widely considered the greatest singles badminton player, winning two Olympic golds and five World Championships.

questions.question: Which player held the World No. 1 ranking for a consecutive 199 weeks between 2008 and 2012?
questions.explanation: Lee Chong Wei of Malaysia held the World No. 1 ranking for a consecutive 199 weeks between 2008 and 2012.

questions.question: In which year was the International Badminton Federation (now Badminton World Federation, BWF) founded?
questions.explanation: The International Badminton Federation (now Badminton World Federation, BWF) was founded in 1934.

questions.question: What is the maximum speed ever recorded for a badminton smash in a professional competition?
questions.explanation: In 2023, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy of India set a world record with a smash speed of 565 km/h (351 mph), making badminton the fastest racket sport.

questions.question: How many players are on the field/ice for each team during a standard field hockey match?
questions.explanation: In field hockey, a match is played by 11 players per team (including the goalkeeper), while ice hockey has 6 players on the ice.

questions.question: Under standard rules, which side of the hockey stick is flat and can be used to hit the ball?
options: A. Both sides are flat and usable
B. Only one specific flat side
questions.explanation: The curved side of the hockey stick is flat on only one side; players must only use this flat side to hit the ball.

questions.question: Which country is the most successful nation in Olympic field hockey history, holding a total of 8 gold medals?
questions.explanation: India is the most successful nation in Olympic field hockey history, winning 8 gold medals, including 6 consecutive golds from 1928 to 1956.

questions.question: What is the projectile used in ice hockey called?
questions.explanation: Ice hockey is played with a hard rubber disc called a puck, while field hockey is played with a solid ball.

questions.question: Who is regarded as the greatest field hockey player ever, famous for his extraordinary stickwork and 3 Olympic Gold medals with India?
questions.explanation: The legendary Dhyan Chand of India is regarded as the greatest field hockey player ever, known for his extraordinary ball control.

questions.question: From which area on the pitch must a field hockey shot be taken to be counted as a valid goal?
options: B. Inside the opponent's 'shooting circle' or 'D'
C. From the penalty spot only
questions.explanation: In field hockey, goals can only be scored from inside the 'shooting circle' or 'D'.

questions.question: Where did ice hockey originate in the early 19th century, with the first indoor game played in Montreal?
questions.explanation: Ice hockey originated in Canada in the early 19th century, with the first indoor game played in Montreal in 1875.

questions.question: Which NHL team holds the record for the most Stanley Cup championships in history (24 titles)?
questions.explanation: The Montreal Canadiens have won the NHL's Stanley Cup a record 24 times.

questions.question: Which legendary ice hockey player, known as 'The Great One', holds almost all major NHL scoring records, including most career goals (894)?
questions.explanation: Wayne Gretzky, known as 'The Great One', holds almost all major NHL scoring records, including most career goals (894) and assists (1,963).

questions.question: What is the duration and period division of a standard field hockey match?
questions.explanation: In field hockey, a match consists of four 15-minute quarters, totaling 60 minutes. Ice hockey is played in three 20-minute periods.

questions.question: Where were field hockey's modern rules first developed in the mid-19th century, establishing Blackheath as the oldest club?
questions.explanation: Field hockey's modern rules developed in England in the mid-19th century, with the Blackheath Football and Hockey Club being the oldest club (1861).

questions.question: In ice hockey, how many players from each team are on the ice during standard play (excluding penalties)?
questions.explanation: In ice hockey, standard play is 5 skaters and 1 goaltender, making 6 players per team on the ice.

questions.question: In which country was the first official Formula 1 World Championship race held at Silverstone in 1950?
questions.explanation: The first Formula 1 World Championship race was held at Silverstone, England (Great Britain) on May 13, 1950.

questions.question: Which two drivers are tied for the record of most Formula 1 World Drivers' Championships, with seven titles each?
questions.explanation: Michael Schumacher and Lewis Hamilton are tied for the record of most World Drivers' Championships, with seven titles each.

questions.question: Which team is the oldest and most successful active team in F1 history, holding the most Constructors' Championships?
questions.explanation: Ferrari is the oldest and most successful active team in Formula 1 history, holding the record for the most Constructors' Championships (16).

questions.question: How many points is awarded to a Formula 1 race winner?
questions.explanation: Points are awarded to the top 10 finishers, with 25 points for 1st place, 18 for 2nd, and 15 for 3rd.

questions.question: Who won the first ever official Formula 1 World Championship race in 1950?
questions.explanation: The first Formula 1 World Championship race was won by Italian driver Giuseppe Farina in an Alfa Romeo.

questions.question: Which legendary Brazilian driver won three World Championships and is famous for his rain mastery and qualifying speed?
questions.explanation: Ayrton Senna of Brazil won three World Championships (1988, 1990, 1991) and is legendary for his sensational qualifying speed and rain-driving mastery.

questions.question: What is the tire compound rule during a dry Formula 1 race?
options: A. Must use the exact same set the entire race
B. Must use at least two different dry tire compounds
C. Can only use Soft tires
D. Tire changes are not allowed unless there is a puncture
questions.explanation: Under F1 rules, drivers must use at least two different dry tire compounds (e.g., Soft and Medium) during a dry race.

questions.question: Who holds the record for the most Grand Prix wins in a single season (19 wins in 2023)?
questions.explanation: Max Verstappen holds the record for the most wins in a single season, winning 19 wins out of 22 races in 2023.

questions.question: Who became the youngest ever Formula 1 World Champion when he won the title in 2010?
questions.explanation: Sebastian Vettel became the youngest ever World Champion in 2010 at the age of 23 years and 134 days.

questions.question: What is the standard minimum race distance of a Formula 1 Grand Prix (excluding Monaco)?
questions.explanation: A Formula 1 race must have a minimum distance of 305 kilometers, which is the least number of laps required to exceed 305 km.

questions.question: What is the maximum time limit for a Formula 1 race in case of suspension or red flags?
questions.explanation: A race must not exceed 2 hours of actual running time, or 3 hours in total duration in case of a suspension.

questions.question: Which driver holds the record for the most consecutive Formula 1 Grand Prix wins (10 wins in a row)?
questions.explanation: Max Verstappen set the record in 2023 by winning 10 consecutive races from Miami to Monza.

questions.question: How many players are on the court for a single team during a standard indoor volleyball match?
questions.explanation: A standard team consists of 6 players on the court.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of touches a team is allowed before sending the ball over the net?
questions.explanation: A team is allowed a maximum of three touches of the ball before sending it over the net.

questions.question: Under standard rules, can a single player touch the ball twice in succession (excluding block touches)?
options: B. No, a player may not touch the ball twice in succession
C. Yes, but only in the front row
D. Only if using their head
questions.explanation: A single player may not touch the ball twice in succession (except when blocking).

questions.question: To how many points is a standard volleyball game played (excluding a deciding fifth set)?
questions.explanation: Games are played to 25 points (using rally scoring), and must be won by a two-point margin.

questions.question: Who invented volleyball in 1895, and what was its original name?
questions.explanation: Volleyball was invented in 1895 by William G. Morgan, a YMCA physical education director, in Holyoke, Massachusetts, originally called 'Mintonette'.

questions.question: In which year and city was Volleyball added to the Summer Olympic games program?
questions.explanation: Volleyball was added to the summer Olympic program in 1964 in Tokyo.

questions.question: To how many points is a deciding fifth set played in a standard volleyball match?
questions.explanation: While standard sets are played to 25, a deciding fifth set is played to 15 points, and must be won by a two-point margin.

questions.question: Which player is the only person to win Olympic gold medals in both indoor and beach volleyball?
options: D. Gilberto Amauri de Godoy Filho (Giba)
questions.explanation: Karch Kiraly of the USA is the only person to win Olympic gold medals in both indoor volleyball (1984, 1988) and beach volleyball (1996).

questions.question: In which year was the Fédération Internationale de Volleyball (FIVB) founded in Paris?
questions.explanation: The Federation Internationale de Volleyball (FIVB) was founded in Paris in 1947.

questions.question: Which women's national team dominated volleyball in the 1990s, winning three consecutive Olympic gold medals (1992, 1996, 2000)?
questions.explanation: Cuba (women) was a powerhouse team, winning three consecutive Olympic golds in the 1990s.

questions.question: What is the specialist defensive player in volleyball called, who wears a different colored jersey and cannot serve or attack?
questions.explanation: The Libero is a specialized defensive player introduced in 1998, who must wear a contrasting jersey and has restrictions on serving, blocking, and attacking.

questions.question: What is the height of the net for men's and women's competition under FIVB rules?
questions.explanation: Under official rules, the net height is 2.43 meters (7.97 ft) for men and 2.24 meters (7.35 ft) for women.

questions.question: In which year was the first FIFA World Cup held in Uruguay?
questions.explanation: The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930, and the hosts won the tournament.

questions.question: Which legendary player is the only one to have won three FIFA World Cups?
questions.explanation: Pelé won three FIFA World Cups with Brazil in 1958, 1962, and 1970.

questions.question: Which country won the most recent FIFA World Cup held in Qatar in 2022?
questions.explanation: Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, defeating France in an iconic penalty shootout after a 3-3 draw.

questions.question: Who is the all-time leading goal scorer in FIFA World Cup history?
questions.explanation: Miroslav Klose of Germany holds the record with 16 World Cup goals scored across 4 tournaments (2002-2014).

questions.question: Who holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history?
questions.explanation: Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history.

questions.question: Which legendary player surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in 2023 to become the NBA's all-time leading scorer?
questions.explanation: LeBron James is the NBA's all-time leading scorer, surpassing Kareem's record of 38,387 points in 2023.

questions.question: Who is the only player in cricket history to score 100 international centuries?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar of India holds this monumental record, with 51 Test centuries and 49 ODI centuries.

questions.question: How many NBA championships did Michael Jordan win with the Chicago Bulls?
questions.explanation: Michael Jordan won 6 championships (two three-peats) with the Chicago Bulls, winning Finals MVP in all six.

questions.question: Which club went an entire 38-game Premier League season undefeated in 2003-04?
questions.explanation: Arsenal's 'Invincibles' went undefeated (26 wins, 12 draws) in the 2003-04 Premier League season under Arsène Wenger.

questions.question: Who is the all-time leading goal scorer in English Premier League history?
questions.explanation: Alan Shearer is the all-time Premier League top scorer with 260 goals.

questions.question: Which manager won the most Premier League titles in history?
questions.explanation: Sir Alex Ferguson won an incredible 13 Premier League titles with Manchester United before his retirement.

questions.question: Which club achieved the historic Continental Treble (Premier League, FA Cup, and Champions League) in 2023?
questions.explanation: Manchester City won the Treble under Pep Guardiola in 2023, replicating Manchester United's 1999 feat.

questions.question: Who holds the record for the most Olympic Gold medals won by any individual in history?
questions.explanation: American swimmer Michael Phelps holds the record for the most Olympic Gold medals in history, with an astonishing 23 golds.

questions.question: Which legendary sprinter achieved the 'triple-triple' of winning 100m, 200m, and 4x100m gold in three consecutive Olympics (2008-2016)?
questions.explanation: Usain Bolt of Jamaica won the 100m, 200m, and 4x100m relay in 2008, 2012, and 2016, cementing his legacy as the fastest human ever.

questions.question: In which city were the first modern Olympic Games held in 1896?
questions.explanation: The inaugural modern Olympic Games were held in Athens, Greece, in 1896, paying homage to the ancient Olympics.

questions.question: Which gymnast holds the record for the most Olympic medals won by an American gymnast?
questions.explanation: Simone Biles is widely regarded as the greatest gymnast of all time and has won 11 Olympic medals (7 gold).

questions.question: The mock obituary that established the 'Ashes' was published in 1882 after Australia defeated England at which ground?
questions.explanation: Australia defeated England by 7 runs at The Oval in August 1882, leading to the famous mock obituary stating English cricket had died.

questions.question: Which player holds the record for the most runs in a single Ashes series (974 runs in 1930)?
questions.explanation: Sir Don Bradman scored an unbelievable 974 runs in just 7 innings during the 1930 Ashes tour of England.

questions.question: Which bowler took a legendary 19 wickets in a single Ashes Test match at Old Trafford in 1956?
questions.explanation: Jim Laker of England set an unbreakable record by taking 19 wickets for 90 runs (9 in first innings, 10 in second) in the 1956 Old Trafford Test.

questions.question: What is historically placed inside the famous Ashes Urn presented to the winners?
options: A. Ashes of a cricket ball
B. Ashes of a wooden bail
C. Soil from the Melbourne pitch
D. A piece of the mock obituary paper
questions.explanation: The Ashes Urn is believed to contain the ashes of a wooden cricket bail, cremated during Australia's 1882 tour.
